error_map.hint: marketstack api key is missing or invalid; re-run pm credentials add marketstack
marketstack rate limit exceeded; the connector will retry with backoff

conformance.reason: ENGINE_GAP (see docs.md Known limits): Marketstack's real date wire shape is a no-colon numeric UTC offset (e.g. 2026-01-02T00:00:00+0000, matching legacy marketstack.go's readFixture stub exactly). cursor_advances re-reads seeded with the max cursor OBSERVED IN FIXTURE RECORDS and formats it via engine/read.go's formatParam->parseLowerBoundTime, which only accepts strict time.RFC3339 (colon-delimited offset) or all-digits Unix-seconds; it hard-errors on +0000. This is a genuine engine limitation, not a fixture-authoring gap -- a colon-offset fixture value would misrepresent Marketstack's actual API response shape. No paritytest/marketstack live substitute exists (this is a hookless Tier-1 bundle); first-sync reads seeded from the RFC3339 start_date config are unaffected, only a repeat-sync re-read seeded with this stream's own emitted cursor value hits the gap.

ENGINE_GAP (see docs.md Known limits): identical to the eod stream's marker -- Marketstack's real date wire shape (no-colon numeric UTC offset, e.g. 2026-08-31T00:00:00+0000) cannot be parsed by engine/read.go's parseLowerBoundTime (strict time.RFC3339 or all-digits Unix-seconds only), which cursor_advances' re-read step requires when formatting the max cursor OBSERVED IN FIXTURE RECORDS. Not a fixture-authoring gap -- a colon-offset value would misrepresent the real API shape. No paritytest/marketstack live substitute exists (hookless Tier-1 bundle); first-sync reads are unaffected.

ENGINE_GAP (see docs.md Known limits): identical to the eod stream's marker -- Marketstack's real date wire shape (no-colon numeric UTC offset, e.g. 2026-02-13T00:00:00+0000) cannot be parsed by engine/read.go's parseLowerBoundTime (strict time.RFC3339 or all-digits Unix-seconds only), which cursor_advances' re-read step requires when formatting the max cursor OBSERVED IN FIXTURE RECORDS. Not a fixture-authoring gap -- a colon-offset value would misrepresent the real API shape. No paritytest/marketstack live substitute exists (hookless Tier-1 bundle); first-sync reads are unaffected.
